mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month payroll employment growth above 150,000 persons signals robust labor market momentum that increases consumer confidence and spending power, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days through higher corporate earnings expectations.
evidence_refs: fact:January 2012 payroll growth of 243k persons indicates labor market acceleration
regime_hint: post-recession recovery with improving labor demand
failure_condition: Rank IC of PAYEMS momentum signal falls below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive payroll months do not produce significantly higher forward returns than negative payroll months (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate held near zero (0.08% as of January 2012) signals an extended monetary accommodation regime that suppresses risk-free rates and forces capital into equities as a yield-seeking mechanism, producing sustained positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.08% in January 2012 reflects zero lower bound policy
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in more than 45% of periods when FEDFUNDS < 0.25%, or Rank IC of the zero-rate signal drops below 0.01 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales (RSAFS) combined with accelerating housing starts (HOUST) indicates strengthening consumer and construction demand that signals broad-based economic expansion, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days through higher corporate revenue expectations.
evidence_refs: fact:December 2011 retail sales at 401.4B and housing starts at 657k suggest demand recovery
regime_hint: early-cycle demand expansion following financial crisis
failure_condition: Joint signal (both RSAFS and HOUST accelerating) produces positive 20-day returns in fewer than 55% of triggered instances, or interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.05) in logistic regression.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth decelerates below 0.5% month-over-month while unemployment remains elevated above 8%, signaling supply-side constraints and potential stagflation risk that produces negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as equity risk premiums widen.
evidence_refs: fact:January 2012 INDPRO at 95.9 with UNRATE at 8.3% reflects post-crisis supply weakness
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with persistent labor slack and supply constraints
failure_condition: Stagflation signal (low INDPRO growth + high UNRATE) produces negative 5-day returns in fewer than 52% of triggered instances, or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPIAUCSL accelerates above 2.5% year-over-year while the federal funds rate remains below 0.25%, creating negative real rates that erode bond returns and drive equity valuations higher through duration compression, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:December 2011 CPI at 227.0 with FEDFUNDS at 0.08% creates negative real rate environment
regime_hint: monetary accommodation with rising inflation expectations
failure_condition: Negative real rate signal produces positive 20-day returns in fewer than 58% of triggered instances, or the interaction between inflation and policy rate is not statistically significant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month period, signaling deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending and corporate earnings, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 8.3% in January 2012 remains elevated from post-crisis peak
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or recession onset
failure_condition: Rising unemployment signal produces negative 60-day returns in fewer than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC of unemployment momentum falls below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured quarterly shows acceleration above 2.5% annualized rate, indicating broad-based economic expansion that supports corporate profit growth and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Q3 2011 real GDP at 13,422.4B reflects post-crisis recovery trajectory
regime_hint: any regime with positive GDP growth
failure_condition: Positive GDP growth periods produce positive 60-day returns in fewer than 60% of instances, or Rank IC of GDP growth signal falls below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) accelerates above 1.0% month-over-month while payroll employment (PAYEMS) grows above 200,000 persons, signaling synchronized supply and labor expansion that reduces unit costs and boosts corporate margins, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 1-month growth > 1.0% AND PAYEMS 1-month growth > 200k persons
evidence_refs: fact:January 2012 INDPRO at 95.9 and PAYEMS at 132.4M show early expansion signals
regime_hint: synchronized supply-side and labor market expansion in recovery phase
failure_condition: Joint supply-labor acceleration signal produces positive 20-day returns in fewer than 62% of triggered instances, or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.05) in logistic regression.
